name: cc-worker
description: cc-worker — the project's second pilot: hand /worker any piece of work (a fix, a change, a feature, a question) and it adapts to the request, reading the goal (roadmap.md) and design (architecture) and reaching for the right cc-pipeline, cc-tools, and its own sibling skills to carry it out. Ships as a /worker command AND a native worker subagent (delegate a whole task to a worker that runs in its own context), both following the worker-rules skill (their shared protocol). Also houses /planner-brainstorm (draw the product's features out of the human, following the planner rules) and /sysdesign-brainstorm (draw the architecture out into a living model.c4 tree, following the sysdesign rules). Three invariants: it keeps the roadmap true (reflects intended work, marks done what it finishes), it changes architecture only through /sysdesign-brainstorm (or the cc-worker:sysdesign rules) first, and it obeys the flags (--auto, --fast, --worktree, --ultracode — how, never what). Depends on cc-pipeline and cc-tools.
